concerns.text: Tasks T015 and T016 (Baseline MAE/RMSE calculation and residual plots) consume the full dataset or cross-validation scores, but the spec's Validation Protocol mandates a strict 80/20 held-out test split. The task to implement this split (T032) is deferred to Phase 6. This creates a semantic ordering violation: T015/T016 produce 'validation' artifacts before the 'validation protocol' (T032) exists to define the test set, rendering the baseline metrics scientifically invalid per the spec.

Task T021 (Wilcoxon signed-rank test) requires 'valid error pairs' from T019.5 (Nested CV). However, T020 trains the 'final_model.pkl' on the 'full training set' without explicitly consuming the held-out test split defined in T032. The statistical comparison (T021) and final model training (T020) are ordered before the data split (T032) is implemented, violating the dependency that the test set must be 'strictly held-out' before any final evaluation or model training on the full set occurs.

Task T026 (SHAP calculation) and T029 (Substructure mapping) depend on the 'final_model.pkl' from T020. However, T020 is trained on the full dataset (pre-split) in Phase 4, while the spec requires validation against a held-out test set. The explainability tasks (Phase 5) are ordered to analyze a model that has not yet been validated against the required test split (T032), creating a flow where the 'interaction zone' is derived from a model state that violates the validation protocol.

Task T032 (Validation Protocol/Split) is placed in Phase 6 (Review Concerns), but it is a foundational prerequisite for the statistical validity of Phase 3 (Baseline), Phase 4 (RF Training), and Phase 5 (Explainability). Moving T032 to Phase 2 (Foundational) or immediately preceding Phase 3 is required to ensure all downstream tasks consume the correct train/test split artifacts.

Task T028 references 'SC-005' for Jaccard similarity stability analysis. The provided spec.md contains no Success Criteria section or SC-005. This task attempts to consume a non-existent artifact/requirement, creating a broken dependency chain. The task must be re-ordered to depend on the explicit definition of SC-005 in the spec or removed if the requirement is absent.

Task T035 requires logging 'measurement uncertainty' and 'quantity of substance' into the quality report. The spec's Data Model only defines SMILES and properties (logP, solubility, boiling point). T035 attempts to consume data fields that do not exist in the upstream data model (T008/T009), creating a semantic dependency on non-existent source fields. This task cannot execute as ordered without modifying the upstream data model or spec.
